title: Agile To Devops Evolution
definition: The transformation of Agile methodologies into DevOps practices, focusing on streamlining development and operations to increase efficiency and value delivery.

proofs.description: Extracted from Dr. Rupert Rebentisch's blog 'Mycelium of Knowledge' — article: 'When AI Becomes Your Zettelkasten's Co-Pilot: How an Agent Changed My Knowledge Workflow'

difficulty_levels.beginner: A journey from Agile (a way of working in software development) to DevOps (integrating development and operations), which makes the whole process faster, more efficient, and customer-focused.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The evolution of Agile methodologies into DevOps, emphasizing continuous integration, delivery, and feedback, aimed at reducing wait times and coordination problems across development and operations.
difficulty_levels.expert: A philosophical shift from Agile (a software development approach) to DevOps (integrating development, operations, and QA), focusing on streamlining the entire value stream, reducing waste, and creating a continuous flow from idea to delivery.

extra_fields.insight: The realization that Agile is not an end goal but a stepping stone towards a more holistic approach in software development — DevOps.
